This is only slow with CanvasKit Chrome. Using Firefox, for example, the issue disappears.
Can we have a priority label on this?

This issue is marked P1 but has had no recent status updates.

The P1 label indicates high-priority issues that are at the top of the work list. This is the highest priority level a bug can have if it isn't affecting a top-tier customer or breaking the build. Bugs marked P1 are generally actively being worked on unless the assignee is dealing with a P0 bug (or another P1 bug). Issues at this level should be resolved in a matter of months and should have monthly updates on GitHub.

Please consider where this bug really falls in our current priorities, and label it or assign it accordingly. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!

I've been looking into whether native text inputs helps resolve this (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120613)--shifting the text rendering to the browser entirely-- but the POC showed the same sluggishness.  @mdebbar do you have any insight here? Seems to only affect Canvaskit on Chrome
If the culprit is `Intl.Segmenter`, maybe we should open a Chrome issue and track from here?

issues.title: Cursor and text field height issues when `TextEditingController` mixes text sizes
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce Run `flutter run` on the code sample below ### Expected results I'd like to be: - Able to customize the height of the cursor - Able to customize the selection height to wrap the entire text - No have the text being vertically cropped ### Actual results There are 3 issues in 1: 1. How to set a cursor that reacts to the different text sizes? `TextField` has the property `cursorHeight` but it applies to the entire `TextField`, I couldn't find a way to customize it per `TextSpan` 2. How to set the selection height that correctly applies to the bigger test styles? I try the different values of `selectionHeightStyle: BoxHeightStyle` but it doesn't seem to be able to wrap the entire test: 3. When the screen is reduced, the text is being cropped vertically (see the video below) ### Code sample
Code sample You can also checkout https://github.com/ValentinVignal/flutter_app_stable/tree/flutter/text-editing-controller-with-different-heights ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(const MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp(home: Scaffold(body: Center(child: MyEditor()))); } } class MyEditor extends StatefulWidget { const MyEditor({super.key}); @override State createState() => _MyEditorState(); } class _MyEditorState extends State { final _controller = MyTextEditingController( 'This is a simple example of indexed words.', ); @override void dispose() { _controller.dispose(); super.dispose(); } @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return TextField(controller: _controller); } } class MyTextEditingController extends TextEditingController { MyTextEditingController(String text) : super(text: text); @override TextSpan buildTextSpan({ required BuildContext context, TextStyle? style, bool withComposing = false, }) { // One every 2 words is using a different style. final words = text.split(' '); final theme = Theme.of(context); return TextSpan( style: style, children: [ for (final (index, word) in words.indexed) TextSpan( text: word + (index < words.length - 1 ? ' ' : (text.endsWith(' ') ? ' ' : '')), style: index.isEven ? theme.textTheme.headlineLarge : theme.textTheme.bodySmall, ), ], ); } } ```

issues.title: Embedding flutter app in div breaks text field visibility on iOS
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this? - [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues) - [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports) ### Steps to reproduce 1) Create a new flutter app with a couple of text fields near the bottom of the page. 2) Load the app into a div on the page using the embedding example. 3) Open the app on chrome or safari on iOS (landscape seems to illustrate the issue best). 4) Tap on the text fields to open the virtual keyboard. 5) Note that if the app is installed in div the text fields remain hidden behind the keyboard. 6) Change the app to load into the body (ie remove the hostElement parameter) 7) Note that now when you tap on the text fields they scroll into view correctly. ### Expected results Text boxes should still scroll into view when the app is embedded inside a div. ### Actual results Text boxes remain hidden when app is installed inside a div. ### Code sample
Code sample ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(const MyApp()); } class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({super.key}); // This widget is the root of your application. @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( title: 'Flutter Demo', theme: ThemeData( colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple), useMaterial3: true, ), home: Scaffold( resizeToAvoidBottomInset: true, body: SingleChildScrollView( child: Column( children: [ Container( height: 500, width: double.infinity, color: Colors.amber, ), TextFormField( initialValue: 'Text 1', ), TextFormField( initialValue: 'Text 2', ), ], ), ), backgroundColor: Colors.cyan, // background color setting ), ); } } ``` ```
```

Screenshots / Video demonstration [Upload media here]
### Logs

issues.title: When inputting Japanese characters using TextField and confirming, the characters are duplicated.
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

When using TextField to input Japanese, part of the characters after conversion are duplicated. For example, entering "日本語を入力します" (inputting Japanese) and confirming with the Enter key results in "日本語を入力します入力します" (inputting Japanese inputting Japanese) with part of the text duplicated.

1. Open a Flutter app containing a TextField widget.
2. Focus on the TextField and input "日本語を入力します" (inputting Japanese).
3. Convert the input to kanji.
4. Confirm the input by pressing the Enter key.

Flutter Version: 3.22.1
Platform: macOS
Development Environment: macOS Sonoma

### Expected results

After confirmation, the TextField should display the input without any duplication.

### Actual results

After confirmation, part of the input is duplicated in the TextField.

### Code sample

...
@override
Widget build(BuildContext context) {
  return TextField();
}

### Screenshots or Video

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

[Upload media here]

</details>

macOS's default Japanese input method includes a feature called "Live Conversion." When "Live Conversion" is enabled, shorter words are converted to kanji more frequently, making the issue less noticeable. Example Input:
1. Turn off IME's "Live Conversion."
2. Input "にほんごをにゅうりょくします".
3. Press space to convert to kanji.
4. Press enter to confirm; the part that was not selected in step 3 gets duplicated.

Google's Japanese Input Method
https://www.google.co.jp/ime/

Hi Contributor
Could you please let me know the estimated time frame for resolving this issue? If it will take too long, I am considering temporarily addressing the problem by adding additional formatting to the Flutter input box. Would this be a feasible solution? Additionally, could anyone provide some good suggestions on this matter?

Unfortunately I can't give you a timeline. No one is assigned to this so no one is actively working on it. I would be happy to review a PR if anyone has ideas, though.

These type of bugs pop up sometimes because Flutter is trying to guess at how all the different IMEs handle composing input ([in the engine](https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/main/shell/platform/darwin/macos/framework/Source/FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm)). Likely we are committing or not committing the composing region at the right time, or something like that.

If you can't find a fix in the engine then I think your formatting solution is probably a good workaround. It will be hacky but you might  be able to get it to work.

Another workaround to try might be using [DeltaTextInputClient](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/DeltaTextInputClient-mixin.html), which will allow you to see exactly what changed between keystrokes. This is definitely still a workaround though. Ideally we can find what's wrong in the engine.

issues.title: The operation of converting from Japanese strings to symbols on certain Android devices is bad
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill out the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->
Using TextField on Samsung devices, the behavior when converting from Japanese to 2-letter symbols is bad.
The same phenomenon did not occur when I used Google Gboard, so I think this is a problem specific to Samsung's software keyboard .


## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. Type "かっこ" in Japanese
3. Tap the line break symbol in the lower right corner of software keyboard. 

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12440462/217768620-9fecc264-7d46-409d-ba5a-614052b52f88.mov

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
Text input is confirmed. Like this word app.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12440462/217769317-ff43990e-4397-45ef-a1dd-8d3fc0afc65b.mov

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
Text input is not confirmed. 



<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyHomePage());
}

class MyHomePage extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Center(
          child: TextField(maxLines: null),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```
comments:
## Triage report I can reproduce this issue on a Samsung S9+ running Android 8
Code Sample (Same as OP) ```dart import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() { runApp(const MyHomePage()); } class MyHomePage extends StatelessWidget { const MyHomePage({super.key}); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return const MaterialApp( home: Scaffold( body: Center( child: TextField(maxLines: null), ), ), ); } } ```

If you run into this issue, please like this issue so we can adjust the priority accordingly.

My understanding of「」(and sometimes []) is that it is treated like emphasis/quotes in Japanese. This thread (https://www.reddit.com/r/LearnJapanese/comments/4qipx9/how_do_i_typewith_the_japanese_samsung_keyboard_d/) seems to imply that these special characters show up in the suggested word list when かっこ is typed, but only on some devices. The suggestion only sometimes appeared on my Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra with the Samsung keyboard, and even then I had to scroll several suggestions deep. So, I am not certain this is a strictly Flutter related issue. I will need to do some additional investigation and may try out a native Android app behavior for comparison.

UPDATE: I quickly tried a sample multiline text field in a Native Android app and it does not exhibit the incorrect behavior when using the Samsung Keyboard.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/233583/221068587-add61816-1e51-43e3-a230-a98a8437af61.mp4

> these special characters show up in the suggested word list when かっこ is typed, but only on some devices.

This suggestion appears because かっこ is a Japanese term for a bracket or parenthesis:
https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/かっこ
https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/括弧#Japanese

That includes the ASCII brackets "[]", which are what I see in the original repro videos, and a variety of others. In Gboard in Japanese, when I type かっこ I get suggestions including [], ［］, 「」, and many more like them. But (with Gboard) I don't reproduce this issue.

I suspect the thing that's tripping up Flutter with the Samsung keyboard is that when it enters that suggestion of a pair of brackets, it puts the caret in between them. That's what happens in both of the videos above of non-repros outside of Flutter; it seems like a nice feature. And then the symptom in the repro looks like the sort of thing that could be caused by confusion about that happening.

That would also explain why the issue occurs with the Samsung keyboard but not with Gboard, because Gboard doesn't do that — in my testing, when you choose a suggestion like "[]", it inserts that and puts the caret at the end of it, as usual.

Debug info is being tracked here (ongoing) - https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wXqJ5kc_PmxK5eTeFZA3C0dKa6vbIAmkQuSTKqEJx5s/edit#

As an FYI, I am only available to work on this periodically as a background task due to other responsibilities requiring my attention, so there may be delays in my updates. If this issue is upvoted significantly and the priority increases, someone else may need to volunteer to take on this work.

This may be a workaround for now until there is a fix. Using the symbol keyboard, you can type in the quotes directly rather than having to rely on the suggestions.
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/233583/224444019-7aced6fa-6268-43b5-afad-2e63257f79ad.mp4

I was able to test out a theory and found a somewhat hacky fix (documented in https://docs.google.com/document/d/1wXqJ5kc_PmxK5eTeFZA3C0dKa6vbIAmkQuSTKqEJx5s/edit#). I'll work to get up a PR for review soon.

> documented in https://docs.google.com/document/d/e/2PACX-1vR7S6cslW4OSyJaFSgCNn3I7yxufrJlQNC-H6HSHzLGhlQyspwP094YTYq9g9n2XdAGZFMIT4MQ6usV/pub

(Is that linked doc meant to be publicly accessible? I get a permission error.)

> > documented in https://docs.google.com/document/d/e/2PACX-1vR7S6cslW4OSyJaFSgCNn3I7yxufrJlQNC-H6HSHzLGhlQyspwP094YTYq9g9n2XdAGZFMIT4MQ6usV/pub
> 
> (Is that linked doc meant to be publicly accessible? I get a permission error.)

It should be public now - https://docs.google.com/document/d/1U-j63rmxA5g5DxWovIHM1PkAehDSGq98fQ78Y7HxMjc/edit?usp=sharing

Very interesting, thanks. I see there that there seems to be a key event with … Hmm, I can't seem to copy-paste from the doc in the normal way; when I try, I get an error banner:
> Options to download, print, and copy have been disabled on this file.

Is that something you can adjust?

In any case, I see in the doc that there seems to be an ACTION_UP key event for KEYCODE_DPAD_LEFT without a previous ACTION_DOWN. That is odd, and sounds like some hacky behavior from the keyboard.

In the repro video, it looks like there's something else odd going on that doesn't seem like it would be explained by that alone, though.
* After hitting "OK", the text is a pair of brackets "[]", and there is no underline indicating that composing is still active.
* But then after hitting "enter", composing resumes, with the string "かっ\nこ".
* So it seems like at the same time as the input was painting "[]" with no composing region, something else (the platform text input plugin inside the engine, perhaps?) had a different idea of what the state was: it thought that there still was an active composing region, containing "かっこ", with the cursor before the こ.

So it would be interesting to understand, after the "OK", what all the different pieces of this subsystem think the state of things is. And to understand what the sequence of steps was from before the "OK" (when everything seemed to agree, correctly, that the state was a composing region of "かっこ" with the cursor at the end) to that situation after the "OK".

The ACTION_DOWN for KEYCODE_DPAD_LEFT is actually being sent but it is being consumed before it reaches the InputConnectionAdapter. When the ACTION_DOWN event is handled directly by the InputConnectionAdapter rather than delegating to the KeyboardManager as with my quick fix, the cursor moves as expected and you can continue to type.


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/233583/225770128-6ef2cfb3-3286-410f-85e4-e067bbaad31c.mp4

Thanks for the repro and the analysis, I'm on Samsung Keyboard 3.3.31.83 but unfortunately I couldn't repro this. To confirm that this is a race between the `commitText` call and the <kbd><-</kbd> key event, could you try adding print statements (e.g., `print('updateEditingValue: $value');`) to `TextInput.updateEditingValue` and `TextInput._setEditingState` ?

> Thanks for the repro and the analysis, I'm on Samsung Keyboard 3.3.31.83 but unfortunately I couldn't repro this. To confirm that this is a race between the `commitText` call and the <- key event, could you try adding print statements (e.g., `print('updateEditingValue: $value');`) to `TextInput.updateEditingValue` and `TextInput._setEditingState` ?

Added log in https://docs.google.com/document/d/1U-j63rmxA5g5DxWovIHM1PkAehDSGq98fQ78Y7HxMjc/edit#heading=h.1o6paqa10jhb

I am more convinced that this is a timing issue. I attached a video that shows when I add non-suspending breakpoints, the system slows down just enough that you can see that the cursor moves left (at ~0:07) before the suggestion replaces the typed text. Just an FYI, I'm not familiar enough with how multithreading works in this case (there's a ton of moving parts), so a proper fix will take some time.


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/233583/232565200-1d50e65d-5e41-4d83-9807-c16dd3c2c4b9.mp4

@cbracken / @LongCatIsLooong I put a breakpoint in `HighlightModeManager.handleKeyMessage` (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/focus_manager.dart#L1780) and noticed that it returns `true` for `handled` for the left arrow key down event that is being sent programmatically by the IME right after the autosuggested text. I think this is why the engine does not execute `InputConnectionAdapter.handleHorizontalMovement` (https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/main/shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugin/editing/InputConnectionAdaptor.java#L339) I'm not familiar with the decisions that went into each of the respective implementations. Should the left arrow key down event be treated as a focus/highlight event in the framework or as a change in selection in the engine?

I added more notes to https://docs.google.com/document/d/1U-j63rmxA5g5DxWovIHM1PkAehDSGq98fQ78Y7HxMjc/edit?usp=sharing per discussion with @LongCatIsLooong. This issue may require a non-trivial refactor in the way the embedder processes input and key events and hands them off to the framework. So, I am handing off this issue to @LongCatIsLooong who will take a closer look in the future.

Interestingly, while I was helping to reproduce https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/125765, I also tested this issue and noticed that the incorrect behavior does not happen for web on Android even with the same Samsung keyboard. In this case, the cursor is moved between the brackets.

Per the discussion, upon selecting the `[]` entry, the IME sends a batch edit that has 2 operations:

<beginBatchEdit() call>

1. commits `[]` which also replaces the composing text
2. sends a <kbd>←</kbd> as a key event

<endBatchEdit() call>

Since batch edits are meant to be atomic operations they're only sent to the framework when `endBatchEdit()` is called which happens after the <kbd>←</kbd> key event, and unfortunately key events are not being treated as part of a batch edit right now.
So the key event arrives at the framework first, and then the text replacement, after the `endBatchEdit()` call.
The `triaged-android` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-android` label or `fyi-android` label.

This issue is assigned to @LongCatIsLooong but has had no recent status updates. Please consider unassigning this issue if it is not going to be addressed in the near future. This allows people to have a clearer picture of what work is actually planned. Thanks!
This issue was assigned to @LongCatIsLooong but has had no status updates in a long time. To remove any ambiguity about whether the issue is being worked on, the assignee was removed.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Run `flutter create example`.
2. Just insert TextField() somewhere in Scaffold Widget.
like 

```
return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: Column(
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: <Widget>[
            TextField(),
            const Text(
              'You have pushed the button this many times:',
            ),
            Text(
              '$_counter',
              style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headline4,
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),

> Looks similar to #120557. @sunjianan9900 can you confirm?

It is indeed the same issue. But the example I gave includes the exception when inputFormatters are present.

Generally speaking when typing Chinese, when the user presses a space, the English symbol (pinyin) will be converted to the corresponding Chinese character. When the user presses the Enter key, the English symbols will be entered directly.

Currently it seems that this truncated input bug manifests itself by automatically and accidentally typing the Enter key. It has nothing to do with what is typed.

Example:

Normal case: nihao [user presses spacebar] => 你好

Occurrence: n [accidentally causes the Enter key to be entered] ihao => ni好 (Since "i" can't spell out any Chinese, "i" will just appear)

@saibotma I believe this is happening because of the `TextField` is not accounting for the characters that have accents/diacritics. If you specify `filled: true, fillColor: Colors.blue.withAlpha(100),` in the TextField's decoration, you will see the accents are outside the field's area. @znromonk I have the same impression. It should behave the same on every platform, though, from my point of view.
I think we can mark this as a P2 for now.  Issue seems to be occurring on both web and desktop and ideally the rendering behavior should be consistent across all platforms.

issues.title: [add-to-app][Android]: App doesn't rebuild widgets if keyboard is opened on older android versions
issues.body:
## Description
If you add a flutter fragment to an existing app, the behavior after the keyboard was opened differs between android versions (8/9 vs. 12). While Android 12 rebuilds the widgets when the keyboard is opened, Android 9 doesn't. Behavior clearly observable by watching the `print` statement of the `viewInset.bottom`: Shows different values when keyboard is opening on Android 12, while showing nothing on Android 9.

This leads to the problem, that the lower text fields will not scroll into view on android 9, because they are still hidden behind the keyboard.

Android 8
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31889204/159942386-3a97005f-7f3c-4a75-8261-69655498957c.mp4
Android 12
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31889204/159942371-767f468f-41a3-4b40-a302-04fbeb105c78.mp4

**Expected results:** 
Opening the keyboard will result in a widget rebuild on any Android version, so that text fields will scroll into view automatically.

**Actual results:** 
Opening keyboard on Android 8 & 9 won't rebuild widgets, so text fields are hidden behind the keyboard.

A minimal working example on [github](https://github.com/DarkMikey/flutter_add_to_app_bottom_padding_issue/tree/main) was created. 
It includes a small flutter fragment + an android example (File > New > New Project > Bottom Navigation Activity).
I followed the instructions provided by the flutter docs

 - [Integrate a Flutter module into your Android project](https://docs.flutter.dev/development/add-to-app/android/project-setup)
 - [Adding a Flutter Fragment to an Android app](https://docs.flutter.dev/development/add-to-app/android/add-flutter-fragment)

_Please ignore the whitespace shown in the videos and in the minimal working example, this is another issue which is tracked in [Flutter add-to-app fragment inserts unnecessary bottom padding when keyboard is open #96520](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/96520)_

<details>
  <summary>Basic example: </summary>

```dart
class MyHomePage extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyHomePage({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    print(MediaQuery.of(context).viewInsets.bottom);
    return Scaffold(
      body: SingleChildScrollView(
        child: Column(
          children: [
            _buildTextField(),
            const SizedBox(height: 30),
            _buildTextField(),
            const SizedBox(height: 30),
            _buildTextField(),
            const SizedBox(height: 30),
            _buildTextField(),
            const SizedBox(height: 30),
            _buildTextField(),
            const SizedBox(height: 30),
            _buildTextField(),
            const SizedBox(height: 30),
            _buildTextField(),
            const SizedBox(height: 30),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }

  Widget _buildTextField() {
    return const TextField(
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        border: OutlineInputBorder(),
        hintText: 'Enter a search term',
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

<details>
  <summary>flutter doctor -v</summary>

issues.title: TextField's underline on text is misaligned on Web when using Japanese Keyboard (ATOK 2014)
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Hit keyboard "a" on TextField then shows "a".
2. Turn on Japanese Input Mode of IME.
3. Hit keyboard "a" then shows "aあ". 
4. Hit keyboard "space key" then "あ" is changed to "阿"(correct) and underline is rendered at "a"(incorrect).

### Expected results

4. Hit keyboard  "space key" then "あ" is changed to "阿" and underline should be rendered at "阿".

### Actual results

4. Hit keyboard "space key" then "あ" is changed to "阿"(correct) and underline is rendered at "a"(incorrect).

This issue occurred on **Web only.**
The result is correct on Windows Application.

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  final TextEditingController _controller = TextEditingController();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: Material(
       child: TextFormField(
         controller: _controller,
       ),
      )
    );
  }

  @override
  void dispose() {
    _controller.dispose();
  }
}

issues.title: Show suffix/prefix text in TextField even when it is not in focus and empty.
issues.body:
Closed related issue: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/19488

@willlarche has closed the issue on 21 Aug 2018 because it was impossible to support both the floating animation behaviour and keeping the suffix/prefix at all times. Since flutter v.1.13.2 in 2020 the floating behaviour got smarter and I believe it should now be possible to always show the suffix when `floatingLabelBehavior: FloatingLabelBehavior.always` is set, since the disabled animation would not interfere with the suffix widget.
comments:
Hi @FufferKS 
To show a suffix/prefix text that needs to be visible when the field is not focused/empty  you just need to use the prefixIcon/suffixIcon parameters on the `InputDecoration`.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/56543#issuecomment-662129426 for more information.

Closing this as TextField is working as intended. If you disagree please feel free to comment and I will reopen it.
Thank you

Left: unfocused, Right: focused
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23334356/91192235-fdf09580-e6f5-11ea-830d-8c05c2824037.png)
I want both of the fields to always display the suffix (and hint when the input is not empty).
Here's a minimum snippet:
```
TextField(
      textAlign: TextAlign.end,
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        floatingLabelBehavior: FloatingLabelBehavior.always,
        suffixText: 'USD',
        labelText: 'Value${widget.label.hashCode % 2}',
        hintText: '0,00',
      ),
    )
```
If I use Your suggestion, then the suffix lands above the input area:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23334356/91192499-48721200-e6f6-11ea-82fe-476d32707a12.png)
As You can see in the inspector, it is because this option probalby reserves space for an entire material icon.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23334356/91192600-6770a400-e6f6-11ea-90e5-223eb4ddcb8b.png)
FYI @HansMuller @guidezpl

Reopening since the statement on https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/64552#issuecomment-680088459 is valid on latest master channel. A possible related issue was closed few months ago https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/56543.

![brun](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/67424416/91267517-75144100-e76b-11ea-9b04-9eed7ec71e35.gif)

<details>
<summary> complete sample code</summary>

```
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      home: Scaffold(
        body: Center(
          child: TextField(
            textAlign: TextAlign.end,
            decoration: InputDecoration(
              floatingLabelBehavior: FloatingLabelBehavior.always,
              suffixText: 'US',
              suffixIcon: Text('USD'),
              prefix: Text('PF'),
              prefixIcon: Text('PF'),
              labelText: 'Value 333',
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```
</details>

<details>

no,no,no need to fix it.

Setting [setExpandedHintEnabled](https://developer.android.com/reference/com/google/android/material/textfield/TextInputLayout#setExpandedHintEnabled(boolean)) to `false` allows to the `prefix` and `suffix` text to be visible when text field is not in focus and empty. | Unfocus | Focused | | --------------- | --------------- | | | | See this [thread](https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android/issues/1459) for more details.

In this case adding a parameter similar to `setExpandedHintEnabled` to `InputDecoration` makes sense to me.
cc: @justinmc
It works in 2023, using `floatingLabelBehavior`

The suffix is not showing when the textfield is not focused till now even when i use:

`floatingLabelBehavior: FloatingLabelBehavior.always`

Same for the prefix attribute. For some reason when the text field is not empty my prefix Widget is remaining visible. But whenever it is empty it disappears when losing focus. My use case is the following: I am using a PopupMenuButton as a prefix Widget to provide the user with different search modes. But since pressing the PopupMenuButton removes the focus from the text field it happens that the PopupMenu remains visible even when the PopupMenuButton disappears given that the input field is empty. Does anyone have a workaround for the moment?

Edit: Okay actually I found a solution: Instead of using the prefix attribute I switched to using the prefixIcon attribute. Of course its semantics does not match given it is not an icon but at least the behaviour is exactly as I'd like it to be. I think a flag to control the visibility of prefix/suffix would be awesome.
Yep, the same issue. Bad behavior or bad name property. Suffix - visible only when focused. SuffixIcon - always visible.

Lucky you. For me prefixIcon workaround isn't working. Any other idea?

I'm using Flutter v3.16.9.

Is this a valid feature request now? I need to show some trailing text, and `suffixIcon` breaks the text alignment.
![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/40336192/bcf34b91-75d5-4a11-8ed1-8b60c8fdea2e)
![image](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/assets/40336192/0c83c058-982c-40e8-8fa7-6609ca4e5062)
How is it possible that in 2024 this will not be solved? The SuffixText - Suffix is only displayed when you focus on it.

Quite needed feature indeed, it seems like a common requirement. The prefix icon and floatingLabelBehavior: FloatingLabelBehavior.always are not working for me as well. 

```dart
     Expanded(
                  child: TextField(
                    decoration: InputDecoration(
                      floatingLabelBehavior: FloatingLabelBehavior.always,
                      hintText: FlutterI18n.translate(context, 'from'),
                      suffixText: "Y.0",
                    ),
                  ),
                ),
```

little trick: 
```
Widget? suffix;

...
 suffix: suffix,
 label: suffix != null
              ? Container(
                  child: Stack(
                    clipBehavior: Clip.none,
                    alignment: Alignment.centerRight,
                    children: [
                      Positioned(
                        right: -16,
                        child: suffix!,
                      ),
                    ],
                  ),
                )
              : null,
 ...
```

@Jeferson505 This is just a workaround, as hint text will be hidden in the presence of label text.
If you don't need hint text for your solution, this will work, but sadly, this isn't what I am searching for.
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

my workaround:
```dart
InputDecoration(
  suffixIconConstraints: const BoxConstraints(),
  suffixIcon: Text(".00"),
)
```

@TahaTesser 
hope you can take a look at this and work your magic.
cc: @bleroux would've better insight in this area.

From my findings, for non Flutter apps, <kbd>Insert</kbd> key is not widely supported. For instance, on Ubuntu, it works on gedit but not on Chrome or Firefox. On macOS, I have not found an application where it works (I don’t have a lot of them, for instance, I tested with VSCode and Android Studio and they do not support this key). On Windows, VSCode, Chrome, Blink do not support it, LibreOffice writer supports it.

Nonetheless, adding a way to optionally activate <kbd>Insert</kbd> on Flutter `TextField` seems a valid requirement.
CC @justinmc who might now if such a feature is already available or on the work or if this is out of scope.

Thanks for doing that research @bleroux.  Flutter currently has no built-in support for an insert mode.  In the meantime, you could probably implement it yourself using [Shortcuts](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Shortcuts-class.html) and then listening to [LogicalKeyboardKey.insert](https://master-api.flutter.dev/flutter/services/LogicalKeyboardKey/insert-constant.html).

If anyone needs support for this, please leave a :+1: reaction to this issue and/or comment with your specific use case below.  I think we could consider adding built-in support for this if we have a consensus on how it should work and where, but for now let's gather feedback and use the workaround with Shortcuts.
The `triaged-desktop` label is irrelevant if there is no `team-desktop` label or `fyi-desktop` label.
Id love to have a overtype mode textfield (ideally with a option to force overtype mode), my usecase would be a hexeditor, where you want to keep the amount of bytes the same, but replace them...

Still that example looks like it doesn't work perfectly. We probable have some work to do here.

@justinmc Thanks for the information. I've tried now, but there is the same problem. 
And I would like to edit not only tag(chip), but also just general text and I would like to delete tag(chip) via backspace.
I understand I can delete tag(chip) of InputChip implementing onDelete callback function though.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db14c073-84f6-43c2-b073-9163668a722a

@justinmc This issue is happening because text editing value is not recognizing UTF16 character. In this case text's length is 6, which should actually be 3 if i'm not wrong. I will give a try to fix this.

Ah, have you seen the [characters API](https://main-api.flutter.dev/flutter/characters/Characters-class.html)?:

```dart
const String string = '😆';
print('for $string, length: ${string.length}, characters: ${string.characters.length}');
```

```
for 😆, length: 2, characters: 1
```

I was looking into code and found that we are using [character](https://pub.dev/packages/characters) package and trying to get  leading or trailing selection with that. I feel in selection phase, something is going wrong but still need to spend some time to understand.

@justinmc I might need your help to solve this. What i have figured out till now is
1. TagEditingController is replacing UTF16 defined by user as WidgetSpan
2. In TextPainter, we convert spans to plainText and based on that we do intents and actions
3. Conversion from WidgetSpan to plainText is internally using PlaceholderSpan's computeToPlainText method which internally is adding object replacement character to buffer, which is not matching actual unicode defined by us.

One solution that comes to my mind is, user can provider unicode in widget span. What do you think ?

@rkishan516 @justinmc Thanks for the investigating. 

> text editing value is not recognizing UTF16 character.

Alright. Then I tried to use UTF8 private use area( `E000~`) instead of `F0000~` I used previously. And it worked as I expected. I found a workaround. Thanks.

Dart's String uses UTF-16, but in text editing value is probably UTF-8. Is my understanding correct?
@sekitaka For the widget span you're using UTF16, it's not decoding value given by you rather just using placeholder object replacement character.
@justinmc Do we have to fix this, or let it go as it is ?

@justinmc I was researching ways how to implement a custom spell checker for the web, using an API. One of the ways I've tried was to just send the contents to gpt-3.5-turbo API to return any misspelled phrases and then find their positions back in the original text (with some optimisations to save requests). That is how I got unsorted and overlapping arrays in some cases.

I hope this will get sorted out when DefaultSpellCheckService will get enabled on the web (#40682)

If you ever plan to do a deeper overhaul, I have two more suggestions:

1. Users expect to see the misspellings underline when they click into to TextField (on focus gain). Currently, they need to start typing.
2. The current design with completing a Future on each character is very unfriendly when using an API (caching, out-of-order)

I think both could be solved if there was a possibility to supply a `Stream<List<SuggestionSpan>>` to SpellCheckConfiguration. This way, developers could call the API and stream the suggestions at their will, including right in initState().

It looks like there are bugs in [buildTextSpanWithSpellCheckSuggestions](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/702b78c6be73eadc1b12c99ea4534ceb8eaee015/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/spell_check.dart#L193) where out of order or overlapping suggestionSpans cause incorrect results.

We should fix that and then try building a custom service that gets results from a fake network API and adding it as an example to the repo.  That could help inform your idea about using a Stream too.

I filed a separate bug for the problem of spell check not happening until typing begins: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/132270
